files_created: projection utilities or tests as needed under src/semantic/ and __tests__/

risks.title: Breaking existing .coderef/index.json consumers by changing ElementData shape too aggressively
risks.mitigation: Add compatibility audit and fixture tests before changing output fields; keep additive fields where possible.

risks.title: usedBy semantics remain confused with forward imports
risks.mitigation: Add explicit reverse-dependency tests where file A imports file B and only B.usedBy includes A.

risks.title: CodeRef IDs become unstable on ordinary line edits
risks.mitigation: Add an option to omit line anchors and test both anchored and unanchored ID modes.

risks.title: populate accidentally writes source headers by default
risks.mitigation: Make header generation opt-in and add a default populate test proving source files are untouched.

risks.title: Duplicate semantic scanner code continues to run in production
risks.mitigation: Deprecate or isolate ast-extractor and route registry/header/enrichment projections through canonical ElementData.

decision_records.decision: Treat the main pipeline scanner and ElementData as the sole production source of semantic truth.
decision_records.rationale: This removes conflicting scanner outputs while preserving semantic registry, headers, and enrichment as projections.

decision_records.decision: Keep semantic-registry.json as a projection, not a replacement for .coderef/index.json.
decision_records.rationale: .coderef/index.json is the canonical machine contract; the registry can optimize query shape without owning source facts.

decision_records.decision: Make source headers optional and off by default.
decision_records.rationale: Headers are useful for humans but should not become agent truth or mutate source during default populate runs.

compatibility_audit.canonical_truth: .coderef/index.json remains the machine truth; semantic-registry.json and source headers are projections.
field_contracts: ElementData.file is emitted as a project-relative POSIX path in generated outputs.
ElementData.codeRefId is a line-anchored canonical CodeRef ID.
ElementData.codeRefIdNoLine is the stable no-line variant.
ElementData.exports, imports, usedBy, related, and rules remain additive fields.
ElementData.usedBy is reverse dependency data: files that import or depend on the current file.

consumer_contracts.contract: Runs the canonical pipeline and projection generators; source headers are opt-in.
Continue to read ElementData with additive semantic fields.
Read query-optimized entries projected from canonical ElementData.
Prefer canonical CodeRef IDs when graph metadata carries them.
Use project-relative POSIX file paths and carry CodeRef ID metadata on element nodes.

constraints: No production populate path may invoke src/semantic/ast-extractor.ts as a second scanner.
Source header writes remain off by default.
Relative TypeScript ESM .js imports resolve back to .ts/.tsx project files for reverse usedBy.
